page: Google Cloud Platform e2-medium is a shared-core virtual machine belonging to the e2 server family. It features 2 vCPUs mapped to a single physical Intel Xeon core with 2 threads, operating at a base frequency of 2.2 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is provisioned with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile. The instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng testing, scoring 2642.19, which places it in the top 25%. However, due to its shared-core architecture, multi-core CPU performance is weak at 2642.78. Memory bandwidth is limited, with read-only large block speeds at 6.36 GB/sec and write speeds at 4.83 GB/sec, both falling into the bottom 10%. Similarly, Passmark CPU and memory scores reflect resource constraints, with a Passmark CPU score of 3105.59 and a memory score of 817.13.

The primary tradeoff of the e2-medium is its shared CPU allocation, which limits sustained multi-threaded performance and memory throughput. This is evidenced by a low static web serving throughput of 0.63 GB/sec and a Redis SET performance of 0.82 million operations per second. Consequently, this instance is not suitable for high-throughput databases, intensive data processing, or complex LLM inference. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for low-intensity workloads, making it appropriate for development environments, lightweight microservices, basic testing, and small-scale background tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform e2-medium is a shared-core virtual machine designed for entry-level workloads. Powered by an x86_64 Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz, it provides 2 vCPUs across 1 physical core and 4.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities in stress-ng tests, but multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are constrained, falling into the bottom 10% to 25% of tested hardware. Specifically, large-block memory read bandwidth is limited to 6.36 GB/sec, and static web serving throughput reaches 0.63 GB/sec. Given these resource limitations, the e2-medium offers a qualitatively cost-effective profile for non-demanding applications. It is best suited for lightweight tasks such as software development, basic testing, small microservices, and low-traffic web serving where sustained high-performance computing is not required.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-medium is a shared-core virtual machine featuring 2 vCPUs, 4 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for lightweight, cost-conscious workloads and testing.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform e2-medium is a shared-core instance with 2 vCPUs and 4 GB RAM, ideal for basic development and low-intensity workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, shared-core virtual machine with two virtual CPUs and four gigabytes of memory for lightweight utility workloads.
bullet_points: Shared-core architecture with 2 vCPUs and 1 physical Intel Xeon core
4.0 GB of system memory with no local storage or GPU accelerators
Strong single-core CPU performance but constrained multi-core capacity
Low memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput
Qualitatively cost-efficient option for non-production environments
Suitable for lightweight microservices, basic testing, and development
